visual_features.overall_style: Very basic, early-2000s academic web style. Primarily browser-default HTML with minimal CSS. Functional, content-first, low-ornament, with a slightly warm scientific feel created by the beige content panel and simple slug illustration logo.
visual_features.visual_hierarchy: Hierarchy is established mostly by typography (bold, italics, underlines) and element size. The slug illustration and site name at the very top are the first focal point, centered and slightly larger. Below that, the main site link 'Web pages of Czech and Slovak malacologists' is bold, blue, and underlined, acting as a primary entry point. The yellow/beige content panel contrasts against the light page background and becomes the main reading area. Inside the panel, the species name in italic bold acts as a subheading, followed by plain body text and then a large photo that dominates the lower half of the viewport.
visual_features.whitespace_usage: Moderate top and side margins around the central content block, but internal spacing is relatively tight. There is a thin band of whitespace separating the site header from the beige content area via a horizontal rule. Inside the beige panel, text sits close to the edges with narrow padding; vertical spacing between lines and sections is minimal, giving a compact, document-like feeling more than a modern web layout.
unique_elements: Black slug illustration logo centered at the top
Bilingual site title (English and Czech) presented in stacked centered lines
Large beige content panel acting as a monolithic article container
Very large species photo embedded as primary visual evidence

layout_characteristics.grid_system: Implicit single-column layout with no evident multi-column grid. Content appears centered with a fixed maximum width approximate to 900–1000px, likely implemented via table or centered block.
layout_characteristics.layout_pattern: Centered single-column with full-width header and a single large content panel. No sidebars or modular grid sections are visible.
layout_characteristics.section_organization: Top header containing logo and site title, followed by a horizontal rule. Beneath that sits one full-width beige section that acts as the entire content area for the species entry, containing breadcrumb, heading, descriptive text, and a large embedded image.
layout_characteristics.alignment: Global alignment is center for header/logo/title and left for the main content. Navigation link text and article text inside the beige panel are left-aligned. Elements align to a common central content column.
responsive_hints: Design appears fixed-width and not responsive; likely built before responsive paradigms. On narrower screens, it would probably cause horizontal scrolling.
No visible evidence of breakpoint-based adjustments or mobile navigation patterns.
layout_characteristics.content_arrangement: Linear top-to-bottom flow. At the top, a slug illustration logo is centered, followed by the site name (one English line, one Czech line) centered below. A horizontal rule then separates the header from the main body. The main body is a single beige panel spanning the content width. Inside the panel, a breadcrumb line ('... > Checklist') appears at the top, then the species name in italic bold, then one or two lines of descriptive body text, followed by a very large centered photograph. There are no side-by-side columns or alternating layouts—everything is stacked vertically in a simple narrative flow.
layout_characteristics.space_allocation: The top header (logo + titles) uses roughly the upper 15–20% of the viewport height, with generous vertical margins above and below the logo. The beige content panel occupies the remaining majority of the screen, heavily dominated by the embedded species image which takes up more than half of the panel height. Text occupies a relatively small vertical portion above the image. Side margins outside the central column are fairly wide, giving the impression of a content column centered in a light background field.
layout_characteristics.content_density: Moderately dense. There is some breathing room around the header and between the header and content, but text within the beige panel is compact with small line spacing and narrow padding from the panel edges. The layout prioritizes fitting text and image into a single scroll viewport rather than creating large editorial whitespace.
layout_characteristics.visual_flow: Strong vertical flow. The eye starts at the top with the contrasting black slug illustration, immediately reads the blue site title, then moves downward through the centered bilingual titles. The thin horizontal rule and color shift to beige create a clear break leading the eye into the content panel. Inside, the breadcrumb subtly precedes the species name, then the descriptive text, and finally the large, vivid image acts as the visual endpoint. There is little horizontal scanning—most movement is straight down the page.

ui_patterns.visual_description: No explicit buttons are visible in the screenshot. Interactions appear to rely solely on text links styled with browser defaults: blue (#0000EE), underlined, bold when used as titles. If buttons were introduced consistent with this style, they would likely be simple rectangular elements with sharp corners (0px radius), minimal or no background color, a 1px grey border (#D0D0D0), and blue link-colored text, closely resembling basic form buttons.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Likely to use native <button> elements or <input type="submit"> within forms, and <a> elements styled minimally as buttons for navigation. No evidence of wrapper divs or complex button structures.

ui_patterns.visual_description: The beige species detail panel functions like a large, single card. It has a full-width rectangular shape with sharp corners (0px radius), a solid background color (#F1D98A approximate), and no visible box shadow; separation from the rest of the page is achieved purely by background color contrast and a small margin top/bottom. Internal padding is modest (approximately 12–16px) with tight vertical spacing between text lines and the image.

ui_patterns.visual_description: No forms are visible in the provided view. In keeping with the rest of the design, any form controls would likely use browser defaults: square-corner text inputs, light grey inner shadows or borders, and standard font rendering. Labels would probably be plain text above or to the left of inputs with minimal spacing.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Expected to use straightforward HTML structures: <form> with stacked <label> and <input> or <select> elements, potentially arranged in a simple table for alignment, reflecting older web practices. Validation feedback would likely be indicated with plain red text near the fields rather than stylized components.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Navigation is text-link based. The main navigation entry visible is a large, bold, underlined blue text line ('Web pages of Czech and Slovak malacologists') centered under the site name, functioning as a primary link. Breadcrumb navigation within the beige panel uses a subtle '... > Checklist' pattern, where 'Checklist' is a blue, underlined text link and the ellipsis and greater-than characters are plain black text. Active states likely rely on browser defaults: unvisited links in bright blue, visited in purple.

  • . Breadcrumbs are likely inline text with an around the 'Checklist' word. No dropdowns or nested menu structures are evident.

ui_patterns.visual_description: No explicit lists are visible in the screenshot. Text is presented in paragraphs with minimal typographic variety. If lists appear elsewhere in the site, they are likely browser-default bulleted or numbered lists with tight vertical spacing and left alignment, using the same serif font as body text.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Expected to use simple semantic HTML lists (<ul>, <ol>, <li>) or even line-broken paragraphs for enumerations, reflecting older academic content publishing habits. No card-based or grid-based list structuring is evident.

heading.visual_style: Classic transitional serif similar to Times New Roman: moderate stroke contrast, sharp terminals, fairly narrow letterforms. Headings rely on bold weight and underlines rather than distinct display fonts.

body.visual_style: Standard browser-default serif body text: readable but compact, with relatively small size and somewhat tight line spacing.

spacing_system.component_padding: Inner padding for the beige content panel appears around 12–16px on all sides, roughly 3–4 base units. Text-to-image spacing is narrow, around 8px (2 base units).
spacing_system.section_margin: Vertical spacing between major sections (e.g., header and beige panel) is around 16–24px (4–6 base units), with smaller 8–12px gaps between text lines and structural elements inside sections.

interaction_hints: Links are clearly indicated by blue underlined text; on hover, they likely retain underlines and may change to a darker blue per browser defaults.
Visited links are likely shown with the default purple color, providing a basic sense of history but no custom active state styling.

No animations or transitions are evident; interactions are strictly instantaneous page loads without micro-interactions. If modernized, subtle fade-ins on image load or hover underlines with slight color transitions (150–200ms) would align with the simple, no-frills tone.
